title: cinchona × erythroderma
intro: Cinchona × erythroderma is a hybrid plant, specifically a cross between Cinchona succirubra and Cinchona pubescens, which are two species of the Cinchona genus. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of quinine, a natural alkaloid that has been used for centuries to treat malaria. The health benefits of Cinchona × erythroderma include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with malaria, as well as its potential to treat other conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Cinchona × erythroderma may be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
